intro: First question: what's your game - cashback or travel? Don't try to do both at once. Pick your lane, grab the best card for it, and move on. Here's my current top 5 for each. I keep this page updated - the sign-up bonuses move around, so this is what's actually best right now.

cashback.why: Best offer ever right now and no annual fee. Easiest $1,000 you'll make. You just need a 'business' (a side hustle / sole trader counts).
Same huge bonus as the Cash, flat 1.5% on everything. Pair it with a Sapphire and those points turn into travel worth way more.
Tiny $500 spend for $250. Lowest-effort win on this list. Great if you don't want to chase a big minimum spend.

cashback.bonus: $200 + all cash back doubled in year one
cashback.why: The beginner card. Cashback Match doubles everything you earn the first year. Easiest approval if you're new to credit.

cashback.bonus: Extra 1.5% on everything (up to $20k) year one
cashback.why: No annual fee, keeps earning UR forever. A solid keeper card once the bonus cards are done.

travel.why: Matches the all-time-high offer. The best travel starter, full stop - transfer points to airlines/hotels for outsized value.
Premium play. Huge bonus + lounges. Only worth the fee if you actually fly and use the credits - do the math.
Massive transferable-points haul on a business card. Stacks with your personal Chase cards for one big UR pile.
Simplest travel card going. Miles are easy to use and Cap One has good transfer partners now. Set and forget.
Increased offer. Free anniversary night every year that easily covers the fee. Great if you do any hotel stays.
